cold_open.hook: Kevin Hassett says AI isn't costing jobs right now—meanwhile tech companies have laid off 262,000 people since 2022.

markets.macro_note: VIX spiked 5.76% despite flat indices—volatility creeping in while the Fed watches inflation expectations jump to 3.7%.

big_story.story_title: Hassett says AI isn't costing anybody their job right now — but tech layoffs keep coming
big_story.angle: The White House's chief economist is gaslighting the room while Discord, Adobe, and Accenture are actively cutting headcount—this is the Crocs IPO of economic messaging: nobody asked for it, it makes no sense, but they're committed.

quick_hits.angle: Babcock & Wilcox beats revenue and books a surge—nuclear energy stocks are having their 'we told you so' moment while the market sleeps.
April home sales disappoint as mortgage rates stay elevated—the housing market has commitment issues, and nobody's buying the rebound narrative.
Discord bundling Xbox Game Pass into Nitro—this is what happens when a platform realizes it can't monetize on vibes alone and chose violence against subscription fatigue.
Sony's piracy lawsuit failure may doom other copyright cases—the internet won, and Hollywood's legal team is still printing cease-and-desist letters to ghosts.

odd_thing.joke_angle: Ships rerouting around the Middle East are now hitting whales—we've successfully created a geopolitical game of bumper cars with marine life.

yesterday_callback.topic: NVIDIA is 'unstoppable' in 2026 according to analysts who said the same thing in 2024, 2023, and 2022—this stock has commitment issues
yesterday_callback.fresh_take: LLY just jumped 4.19% today while NVDA only gained 3.07%—the AI narrative is finally diversifying, and the Mag 7 's stranglehold is loosening.

sign_off.callback_target: Kevin Hassett's 'AI isn't costing jobs' claim—circle back to the 262,000 layoffs as the final word.
